message:
Implement sum(), sorted(), all(), any(), replace(), reversed()

Agent 2: Implemented 4 built-in functions in builtins.zig
- sum(list) → accumulator loop, returns total
- sorted(list) → allocator.dupe + std.mem.sort, returns sorted copy
- all(list) → short-circuit check, returns true if all truthy
- any(list) → short-circuit check, returns true if any truthy

All use block expressions (blk: { ... }) for value returns.
Memory: sorted() allocates (caller must free).

Agent 3: Implemented 2 functions + analyzer updates
- text.replace(old, new) → std.mem.replaceOwned()
- reversed(list) → allocator.dupe + std.mem.reverse()
- Updated analyzer.zig to detect replace/reversed for allocator setup

Memory: Both allocate (caller must free - TODO: defer tracking).

Testing:
- replace() tested successfully (works, has expected leak)
- sum/sorted/all/any/reversed blocked by pre-existing type inference bug
  (line 116 in native_types.zig infers all lists as PyObject)

Placeholders reduced: 15 → 7 (53% reduction!)

File sizes:
- main.zig: 810 lines (⚠️ 10 over 800 limit - needs routing extraction)
- All others under 400 lines

Build: Compiles clean, ready for type inference fix
